message.content:
**Topics:** 2025 mobile game user acquisition (UA) market evolution, quarterly performance dynamics, genre-specific scale and monetization divergence, cross-platform (Android vs. iOS) marketing strategies, digital ad budget allocation, channel diversification, creative optimization, and advertising measurement maturity/attribution modeling.
**Entities:** Mobile game advertisers/marketers, top brands/publishers, Singular (data provider/report publisher), Android, iOS, Paid Social platforms, and game genres (casual, hybrid-casual, mid-core, strategy).
**Dates/Timeframes:** Full year 2025, with granular quarterly breakdowns (Q1–Q4), H2 focus, and the Q4 holiday season. Strategic insights extend into 2026.
**Definitions/Concepts:** Market transition from an "emergency state" to "self-discipline and standardization"; "rational growth path" prioritizing sustainable value over blind expansion; value-centric optimization methodology (aligning creative, bidding, and channels to early payment propensity signals); channel over-concentration risk; attribution frameworks (Last-Click vs. Multi-Touch Attribution/MTA); Assist Value, Cross-Channel Impact, Modeled Conversions, Hybrid Performance Metrics, and iOS privacy-driven Signal Loss.
**Procedures/Strategies:** Cautious budget allocation and efficiency-first testing; shifting optimization targets from raw downloads to user payment propensity; treating Android and iOS as distinct environments with customized creatives, bidding, and evaluation; implementing channel diversification as a risk management necessity; establishing rapid creative testing workflows (prioritizing short-form/UGC over static content) with high-frequency refresh cycles; adopting MTA and modeled conversions to allocate budgets across the full funnel and align internal stakeholders.
**Claims:** The 2025 UA market stabilized with low single-digit YoY ad spend growth, shifting toward precise, measured expansion. Seasonal fluctuations became highly predictable: Q1 focused on efficiency, Q2 showed early recovery, Q3 delivered significant install rebounds, and Q4 broke historical norms by maintaining/improving ROI despite holiday bidding pressure. Casual/hybrid-casual genres dominate install volume, while mid-core/strategy genres drive revenue despite 30%–50% higher CPI, offset by superior conversion rates and LTV. Paid social remains a core pillar but requires diversification to mitigate concentration risk. Advanced measurement and MTA are critical for navigating iOS signal loss and valuing top/mid-funnel activities. Core strategic takeaways for 2025–2026 emphasize systematic growth over aggressive scaling, continuous creative iteration over channel expansion, and reliance on robust internal measurement systems.
**Metrics:** Low single-digit YoY ad spend growth; quarterly MoM/QoQ install fluctuations; Q4 ROI stability/improvement; mid-core/strategy CPI 30%–50% higher than casual; Android UA costs 40%–60% lower than iOS; diversified advertisers outperform single-channel peers by mid-single-digit percentages; ROAS decay cycles tied to creative refresh rates; assist value and cross-channel impact indicators.
**Evidence Types:** Quarterly industry trend analysis, empirical market and platform comparisons, advertiser group performance analysis, and observational studies on privacy constraint adaptation.
**Tables/Figures:** References a figure titled "2025年游戏获客：真实变革何在" and a data visualization titled "2025年广告预算流向解析". All underlying data is sourced from the "Singular 2025 Quarterly Trends Report" (including H2 Rebond data).

**Topics:** 2025 mobile game user acquisition (UA) market evolution, quarterly performance dynamics, genre-specific scale and monetization divergence, cross-platform (Android vs. iOS) marketing strategies, digital ad budget allocation, channel diversification, creative optimization, and advertising measurement maturity/attribution modeling.
**Entities:** Mobile game advertisers/marketers, top brands/publishers, Singular (data provider/report publisher), Android, iOS, Paid Social platforms, and game genres (casual, hybrid-casual, mid-core, strategy).
**Dates/Timeframes:** Full year 2025, with granular quarterly breakdowns (Q1–Q4), H2 focus, and the Q4 holiday season. Strategic insights extend into 2026.
**Definitions/Concepts:** Market transition from an "emergency state" to "self-discipline and standardization"; "rational growth path" prioritizing sustainable value over blind expansion; value-centric optimization methodology (aligning creative, bidding, and channels to early payment propensity signals); channel over-concentration risk; attribution frameworks (Last-Click vs. Multi-Touch Attribution/MTA); Assist Value, Cross-Channel Impact, Modeled Conversions, Hybrid Performance Metrics, and iOS privacy-driven Signal Loss.
**Procedures/Strategies:** Cautious budget allocation and efficiency-first testing; shifting optimization targets from raw downloads to user payment propensity; treating Android and iOS as distinct environments with customized creatives, bidding, and evaluation; implementing channel diversification as a risk management necessity; establishing rapid creative testing workflows (prioritizing short-form/UGC over static content) with high-frequency refresh cycles; adopting MTA and modeled conversions to allocate budgets across the full funnel and align internal stakeholders.
**Claims:** The 2025 UA market stabilized with low single-digit YoY ad spend growth, shifting toward precise, measured expansion. Seasonal fluctuations became highly predictable: Q1 focused on efficiency, Q2 showed early recovery, Q3 delivered significant install rebounds, and Q4 broke historical norms by maintaining/improving ROI despite holiday bidding pressure. Casual/hybrid-casual genres dominate install volume, while mid-core/strategy genres drive revenue despite 30%–50% higher CPI, offset by superior conversion rates and LTV. Paid social remains a core pillar but requires diversification to mitigate concentration risk. Advanced measurement and MTA are critical for navigating iOS signal loss and valuing top/mid-funnel activities. Core strategic takeaways for 2025–2026 emphasize systematic growth over aggressive scaling, continuous creative iteration over channel expansion, and reliance on robust internal measurement systems.
**Metrics:** Low single-digit YoY ad spend growth; quarterly MoM/QoQ install fluctuations; Q4 ROI stability/improvement; mid-core/strategy CPI 30%–50% higher than casual; Android UA costs 40%–60% lower than iOS; diversified advertisers outperform single-channel peers by mid-single-digit percentages; ROAS decay cycles tied to creative refresh rates; assist value and cross-channel impact indicators.
**Evidence Types:** Quarterly industry trend analysis, empirical market and platform comparisons, advertiser group performance analysis, and observational studies on privacy constraint adaptation.
**Tables/Figures:** References a figure titled "2025年游戏获客：真实变革何在" and a data visualization titled "2025年广告预算流向解析". All underlying data is sourced from the "Singular 2025 Quarterly Trends Report" (including H2 Rebond data).
